questions.question: Which keyword creates a class in TypeScript?
options: A) struct
B) type
C) class
D) object

questions.question: What is the default access modifier for class members in TypeScript?
options: A) private
B) protected
C) public
D) internal

questions.question: What does the `private` modifier do?
options: A) Makes the member accessible from anywhere
B) Makes the member accessible only within the class itself
C) Makes the member accessible in subclasses
D) Makes the member read-only

questions.question: What does the `protected` modifier do?
options: A) Makes the member accessible only within the class itself
B) Makes the member accessible within the class and its subclasses
C) Makes the member accessible from anywhere
D) Prevents the member from being overridden
A) A parameter that is converted to a regular function argument
B) A shorthand that declares and initializes a class property directly from a constructor parameter
C) A parameter that is optional
D) A parameter with a default value

questions.question: What does `extends` do in a class declaration?
options: A) Implements an interface
B) Creates a subclass that inherits from a parent class
C) Creates a copy of the class
D) Adds static methods to the class

questions.question: What does `implements` do in a class declaration?
options: A) Inherits methods and properties from another class
B) Ensures the class satisfies the contract defined by an interface
C) Creates an abstract class
D) Makes the class final

questions.question: What is an abstract class?
options: A) A class with no properties
B) A class that cannot be instantiated directly and may contain abstract methods
C) A class that is automatically optimized by the compiler
D) A class with only static methods
A) No error
B) Compile error: Cannot create an instance of an abstract class
C) Runtime error
D) Warning only

questions.question: What must a subclass of an abstract class do?
options: A) Nothing special
B) Implement all abstract methods defined in the abstract class
C) Only override the constructor
D) Declare itself as abstract too

questions.question: What does `super()` do in a constructor?
options: A) Calls the current class's constructor again
B) Calls the parent class's constructor
C) Creates a super user
D) Resets all properties to their defaults
A) 'Rex makes a sound'
B) 'Rex barks'
C) undefined
D) Compile error

questions.question: Can a class implement multiple interfaces?
options: A) No, only one interface per class
B) Yes, using: class Foo implements A, B { }
C) Only with multiple 'implements' keywords
D) Only abstract classes can implement multiple interfaces
A) No error, prints 'hidden'
B) Compile error: Property 'secret' is private and only accessible within class 'Person'
C) Runtime error
D) Prints undefined

questions.question: What is the difference between TypeScript's `private` and JavaScript's `#` private fields?
options: A) They are identical
B) TypeScript's `private` is compile-time only; `#` fields are enforced at runtime
C) `#` fields are only for methods, not properties
D) TypeScript's `private` is enforced at runtime; `#` is compile-time only
A) The factory pattern
B) The singleton pattern using a private constructor
C) The observer pattern
D) An abstract class

questions.question: What are getters and setters in TypeScript classes?
options: A) Special methods that retrieve and store data from a database
B) Accessor methods that control how a property is read and written
C) Static methods for creating instances
D) Methods that validate constructor parameters
C) undefined
D) Compile error

questions.question: What is a static method?
options: A) A method that cannot be changed
B) A method that belongs to the class itself, not instances of the class
C) A method that is called automatically on creation
D) A method that is only available in abstract classes

questions.question: What does `readonly` do on a class property?
options: A) Makes the property private
B) Makes the property assignable only in the constructor or at declaration
C) Makes the property static
D) Makes the property optional
A) No error
B) Compile error: User does not implement serialize()
C) Runtime error
D) Warning about missing method

questions.question: Can a subclass access a `protected` member of its parent?
options: A) No, protected members are not accessible in subclasses
B) Yes, protected members are accessible in the class and its subclasses
C) Only if the subclass is in the same file
D) Only through a getter method
A) 'Hello from Base'
B) 'Hello from Base and Child'
C) 'and Child'
D) Compile error

questions.question: How many classes can a TypeScript class extend?
options: A) Unlimited
B) Two
C) One (single inheritance only)
D) Zero; TypeScript does not support inheritance
A) It is required to override any method
B) It explicitly indicates a method overrides a parent method, helping catch errors if the parent method changes
C) It makes the method final so it cannot be overridden further
D) It calls the parent method automatically
